note: Confirmed/sworn DAG March 2025 and still nominally holds the title (no successor DAG named). Became Acting Attorney General on 2026-04-02 after Trump fired Pam Bondi; nominated for permanent AG in June 2026.
Confirmed Oct 2025; assumed office 2025-11-14. April 5, 2026 reports of a possible Dhillon promotion / Woodward demotion remain unconfirmed.
Confirmed by the Senate 52-45 on 2025-04-04, took office same day.
9th FBI Director; sworn in 2025-02-20; confirmed serving as of June 2026.
Sworn in July 2025 after Senate confirmation; still serving (DEA statement dated 2026-06-25).

jurisdiction: Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives
note: Career ATF agent; Senate-confirmed ~2026-04-29 and sworn in 2026-04-30; previously ATF Deputy Director since April 2025.
13th USMS Director; Senate-confirmed 2025-07-31, sworn in 2025-08-01. Career law-enforcement official.

role: Director of the Bureau of Prisons (DOJ)
note: Appointed by President Trump and sworn in 2025-04-21; former West Virginia corrections commissioner. BOP Director is not a Senate-confirmed position.

Michael Faulkender (16th Deputy Secretary, confirmed 2025-03-26) resigned 2025-08-22. Treasury announced (SB-0479, 2026-04-29) Brooke would perform the delegable duties of Deputy Secretary; also nominated to the post 2026-05-11 (pending). Concurrently Acting Under Secretary for International Affairs.

Office churned through several acting heads in 2025 (Gary Shapley, Michael Faulkender ~Apr 2025); Billy Long sworn in 2025-06-16 as 51st Commissioner, removed 2025-08-08, after which Treasury Secretary Bessent took over as acting. IRS.gov still lists him as 'Commissioner (Acting)' mid-2026; Senate Finance Democrats argue his statutory acting term expired 2026-03-06.
Career/non-partisan official; assumed office September 2023, retained under the Trump administration. Still in role per HSGAC Senate letter dated 2026-04-17.
46th Treasurer; former Georgia (Republican) state senator; named by Trump 2025-03-23. Position no longer requires Senate confirmation. Start date varies by source.

jurisdiction: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
note: Sworn in as the 32nd Comptroller of the Currency on 2025-07-15.

role: Under Secretary for Terrorism and Financial Intelligence (Treasury)
note: Confirmed by the Senate 51-47 in July 2025 (Treasury announcement dated 2025-07-23), succeeding acting head Brad Smith.
Confirmed by the Senate 2025-03-24; took office 2025-03-25. Principal Deputy Secretary (the No. 2 official at State).

role: Deputy Secretary of State for Management and Resources
note: Second deputy secretary position. SFRC advanced nomination 16-6 on 2025-03-12; assumed office 2025-05-19.

role: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations
note: 32nd U.S. Permanent Representative to the UN. Confirmed 47-43 on 2025-09-19; sworn in 2025-09-20. Previously Trump's National Security Advisor.

jurisdiction: U.S. Agency for International Development (functions merged into U.S. Department of State)
note: USAID was dismantled in 2025: Secretary of State Rubio was designated Acting Administrator in early Feb 2025 and oversaw the wind-down. USAID officially ceased implementing foreign assistance 2025-07-01, with remaining functions absorbed into the State Department (NPR, 2025-07-01). No Senate-confirmed USAID Administrator was installed.
Cerberus co-founder; confirmed 59-40 on 2025-03-14, sworn in 2025-03-17 as 36th Deputy Secretary of Defense.
26th Secretary of the Army; confirmed and sworn in 2025-02-25. Reported clashes with SecDef Hegseth (Washington Post, 2026-04) but remains in office as of June 2026.
Under Secretary of the Navy (sworn 2025-10-03) elevated to acting SecNav after SecDef Hegseth removed John Phelan (79th SecNav) on 2026-04-22 (Military Times/CNN/Al Jazeera 2026-04-22/23).
27th Secretary of the Air Force (also oversees the Space Force); former Principal Deputy Director of the NRO. Start date approximate (confirmed/sworn May 2025).

role: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
note: 21st CJCS; recalled from retirement after Trump fired Gen. C.Q. Brown Jr. in Feb 2025. Sworn in 2025-04-11.

role: Vice Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
note: 13th VCJCS (USMC); confirmed 2025-09-19, sworn in 2025-10-01, succeeding Adm. Christopher Grady.

role: Acting Chief of Staff of the Army
note: Vice Chief of Staff of the Army (40th, since 2026-02-06) became acting CSA after SecDef Hegseth removed Gen. Randy George (41st CSA) on 2026-04-02. No Senate-confirmed permanent successor reported as of June 2026.
34th CNO, sworn in 2025-08-25; followed Adm. Lisa Franchetti's removal (Feb 2025) and Adm. James Kilby's acting tenure.

role: Chief of Staff of the Air Force
note: 24th CSAF; confirmed 2025-10-30, sworn in 2025-11-03, succeeding the retiring Gen. David Allvin.
2nd Chief of Space Operations, in office since 2022-11-02; still serving per April 2026 Space Symposium and official bio.
39th Commandant of the Marine Corps, in office since 2023-09-22; still serving as of June 2026.

Sworn in as 9th Deputy Secretary on 2025-03-08; nomination to be Ambassador to El Salvador withdrawn 2026-04-13, remained Deputy Secretary. Serves under Secretary Markwayne Mullin (confirmed 2026-03-24).
U.S. Senate confirmed 2025-06-18; sworn in shortly after.

Acting director since ~2026-06-01, succeeding Todd Lyons (Mar 2025-May 2026). On 2026-06-27 Trump named Lance Schroyer as nominee for permanent ICE Director. ICE has had no Senate-confirmed director since 2017.
Senate-confirmed; began service as Director on 2025-07-15.

role: TSA Administrator, Acting (Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator) (DHS)
note: Named Deputy Administrator April 2025; serving as Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator through at least mid-2026. Trump nominated David Cummins as permanent TSA Administrator (~2026-05-11), pending Senate confirmation.

role: FEMA Administrator, Acting (Senior Official Performing the Duties of the Administrator) (DHS)
note: Career FEMA official (Region 9 Administrator since 2015) serving as acting head from ~May 2026 amid repeated 2025-2026 leadership turnover (Cameron Hamilton, David Richardson, et al.). Trump nominated Cameron Hamilton (~2026-05-11) as permanent Administrator. FEMA has had no Senate-confirmed administrator under the second Trump term.

Executive Assistant Director for Cybersecurity serving as acting CISA Director since ~2026-02-26, after Madhu Gottumukkala was reassigned within DHS. Nominee Sean Plankey withdrew in April 2026; no Senate-confirmed director.
28th Director; the Secret Service Director is a presidential appointment that does not require Senate confirmation.
Uniformed officer (Admiral). Served as Acting Commandant 2025-01-21 to 2026-01-15, then sworn in as the 28th Commandant on 2026-01-15.

role: Deputy Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS)
jurisdiction: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
note: Nominated by Trump on 2026-06-25; not yet Senate-confirmed. Office vacant since predecessor Jim O'Neill (Deputy Secretary June 2025-Feb 2026) departed ~2026-02-13 to lead NSF. Klomp currently serves as HHS chief counselor and head of Medicare.

jurisdiction: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
note: Serving as Acting CDC Director concurrently with his role as NIH Director. Predecessor Susan Monarez (confirmed July 2025) was ousted 2025-08-27; Jim O'Neill acted Aug 2025-Feb 2026 before leaving for NSF.

role: Acting Commissioner of Food and Drugs / FDA Commissioner (HHS)
note: Elevated from FDA Deputy Commissioner for Food to Acting Commissioner after Marty Makary resigned/was forced out ~2026-05-12. No permanent nominee confirmed as of 2026-06-29 (Heidi Overton reported as a leading candidate).
18th NIH Director; Senate-confirmed 2025-03-25, took office 2025-04-01. Also serving as Acting CDC Director since 2026-02-18.

role: Administrator, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (HHS)
note: 17th CMS Administrator; Senate-confirmed 2025-04-03 (53-45), assumed office 2025-04-08.

role: Acting Surgeon General of the United States (HHS)
jurisdiction: Office of the Surgeon General, U.S. Public Health Service
note: OSG senior adviser carrying out Surgeon General duties on an interim basis (announced ~2026-05-19/20). Pending Senate nominee is Nicole Saphier (nominated 2026-04-30). Prior nominees withdrawn: Casey Means (2026-04-30) and Janette Nesheiwat (2025).

role: NOAA Administrator / Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere (Commerce)
note: Nominated Feb 2025; Senate-confirmed early Oct 2025 (reported Oct 7-8, 2025). Previously acting NOAA administrator in first Trump term.

role: Performing the Duties of the Director, U.S. Census Bureau (Commerce)
note: Title is 'Performing the Duties of Director' (acting); no Senate-confirmed director. Announced via Sept 19, 2025 memo, taking over from acting director Ron Jarmin (who followed the Feb 2025 departure of director Robert Santos).

role: NIST Director / Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Technology (Commerce)
jurisdiction: National Institute of Standards and Technology
note: Nominated Oct 2025; Senate-confirmed 46-45 on 2026-05-19. 18th NIST Director; former dean of engineering at Purdue University.

role: USPTO Director / Under Secretary of Commerce for Intellectual Property (Commerce)
note: Nominated March 2025; Senate-confirmed 51-47 on 2025-09-18 (en bloc); USPTO welcome announcement dated 2025-09-22.

Career NPS official (former comptroller); acting director since 2025-01-20. Trump nominee Scott Socha (nominated 2026-02-11) was withdrawn 2026-04-28, so no Senate-confirmed director is in place as of 2026-06-29.
Former NM GOP congressman and state party chair; Senate-confirmed 46-43 on 2026-05-18, sworn in 2026-05-19 as the 20th BLM Director. Sometimes formally listed as 'Stevan Pearce.'
Senate-confirmed 51-47 on 2025-10-07; assumed office 2025-11-18 as the 19th USGS Director (succeeding David Applegate).

role: Chief of the U.S. Forest Service (USDA)
note: 21st Chief; named by Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins on 2025-02-27 and sworn in 2025-03-10, succeeding retiring Chief Randy Moore. Non-PAS agency-head post; first chief appointed from outside the agency.

role: Acting Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (Labor)
note: Career Deputy Commissioner serving as Acting Commissioner since early Aug 2025, after Trump fired confirmed Commissioner Erika McEntarfer on 2025-08-01. First replacement pick E.J. Antoni (nominated ~2025-08-11) was withdrawn ~Sept 2025.

role: Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (Labor) — nominee
note: Career BLS economist nominated by Trump in late Jan 2026 after the E.J. Antoni nomination was withdrawn. Senate HELP Committee vote set for 2026-06-24; no source confirms full Senate confirmation as of 2026-06-29.

role: NNSA Administrator / Under Secretary for Nuclear Security (DOE)
note: Former U.S. Rep. (NY-22); Senate-confirmed 2025-09-18 in a party-line block vote. The Under Secretary for Nuclear Security also serves as NNSA Administrator.
Former Republic Airways CEO; Senate-confirmed 53-43 on 2025-07-09; FAA welcomed him as Administrator 2025-07-10.
Former NHTSA Chief Counsel (2017-2021); Senate-confirmed on 2025-09-18. Peter Simshauser had been Acting Administrator prior.

role: Under Secretary of Education (also performing delegated duties of Deputy Secretary)
note: No Senate-confirmed Deputy Secretary of Education (Penny Schwinn's nomination withdrawn 2025-09-03). Kent confirmed as Under Secretary 50-45 on 2025-08-01, sworn in 2025-08-04, holds delegated authority to perform Deputy Secretary functions. A March 2025 EO targeted the Department for dismantlement, but Congress has not abolished it.

11th Deputy Secretary of VA; Senate-confirmed 2025-03-27, sworn in 2025-03-28 by Secretary Doug Collins. As of early 2026 also performing the delegable duties of Assistant Secretary for Information and Technology / CIO.
